title: Lunathyrium henryi
intro: Lunathyrium henryi, also known as Henry's Lunathyrium, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Lunathyrium henryi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Lunathyrium henryi is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as lady ferns. This classification is based on its leaf morphology, habit, and other characteristics. Lunathyrium henryi is further classified within the genus Lunathyrium, which is a monotypic genus, containing only this single species. The family Athyriaceae is part of the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse order of ferns. Within the order Polypodiales, Athyriaceae is one of the smaller and less diverse families.
uses: Lunathyrium henryi, a species of fern, is used in traditional medicine in some parts of Asia.
